description: 404 Reservoir Not Found is an interactive reservoir-engineering copilot built for the G42 Agentathon. It provides a preloaded local demo dashboard, natural-language reservoir chat, specialist tools for profiles, WCT, pressure, TRAN/connectivity and integrated diagnosis, critic-based hypothesis ranking, an LLM-backed final answer writer for cleaner user-facing explanations, and auditable agent interaction traces.

environment_variables.name: COMPASS_API_KEY
environment_variables.description: Compass API key provided by the evaluation environment, if required by the hosted evaluator.

environment_variables.name: OPENAI_API_KEY
environment_variables.description: Optional local OpenAI API key if external LLM-backed reasoning is enabled.

environment_variables.name: OPENAI_MODEL
environment_variables.description: Optional model name for the V800 LLM Copilot Planner.

environment_variables.name: V800_LLM_MODEL
environment_variables.description: Optional override model for the V800 planner.

environment_variables.name: V800_DISABLE_LLM
environment_variables.description: Set to 1 to force deterministic fallback without LLM planner.

message.description: Alternative field for the reservoir-engineering question.

agents.name: LLMCopilotBrainV800
agents.role: Semantic planner for general chat, reservoir routing and tool-plan generation.

agents.name: EvidencePlannerV800
agents.role: Builds the reservoir evidence plan for specialist tools.

agents.name: DynamicProfileAgentToolV800
agents.role: Generates simulated-vs-observed production and pressure profile evidence.

agents.name: WCTBiasDiagnosticAgentToolV800
agents.role: Analyses water-cut bias, water mismatch and spatial water behaviour.

agents.name: IntegratedReservoirDiagnosisToolV800
agents.role: Combines profile, pressure, WCT and property evidence into integrated diagnosis.

agents.name: ReservoirCriticAgentV807
agents.role: Ranks hypotheses using structured numeric scores when available; otherwise falls back to evidence weighting.

agents.name: UserFacingAnswerPolisherV803
agents.role: Removes internal tool names from the main answer while preserving audit traces.

agents.name: VisualEvidenceStabilizerV806
agents.role: Deduplicates and stabilizes visual evidence blocks.

agents.name: FinalVisualSelectorV810
agents.role: Selects the most appropriate final visual evidence for the question.

agents.name: LLMFinalAnswerWriter
agents.role: Uses the configured Compass/OpenAI-compatible LLM endpoint to rewrite validated diagnostic evidence into a concise user-facing reservoir-engineering answer.

capabilities: General direct-answer mode when LLM is configured
Dynamic routing between direct answer, single specialist tool and multi-agent diagnosis
Oil, gas, water, WCT and BHP history-match review
Invalid well guard and closest-well suggestions
Final visual selection based on user intent
LLM-backed final answer writing for cleaner reservoir-engineering explanations
User-facing language cleanup without modifying diagnostic evidence
Separation between specialist tool evidence, visual panels and final written synthesis

notes.demo_data: The repository includes precomputed demo artifacts under artifacts/ and bundled sample data where present. The evaluator does not need to import reservoir input data before running the demo.
notes.demo_startup: Run python run.py, then open /demo-dashboard or use POST /run.
notes.official_examples: Four official examples are included: three diagnostic multi-agent runs and one invalid-well guard/fallback run.
notes.v700_copilot_brain: V700 Copilot Brain handles general chat/direct answers and reservoir tool orchestration. For reservoir questions it calls real specialist runtime tools and records interaction_edges at runtime.
notes.general_chat: Simple general inputs such as 5+7 are answered directly and not forced into reservoir workflows.
notes.real_interaction_edges: Official examples include V700 runtime interaction_edges generated during tool calls.
notes.v800_llm_copilot_planner: V800 uses an LLM planner when configured to answer general questions directly and route imported-model reservoir questions to specialist tools.
notes.general_chat_requires_llm: Arbitrary general questions require OPENAI_API_KEY or COMPASS_API_KEY plus COMPASS_BASE_URL. Without an LLM key, deterministic fallback is used.
notes.llm_final_answer_writer: The app includes app/final_answer_synthesizer_llm.py. It uses the configured Compass/OpenAI-compatible endpoint to polish final answers after specialist tools and critic validation. If no LLM key is available, fallback behaviour preserves app stability.

name: 404 Reservoir Not Found
display_name: 404 Reservoir Not Found - Reservoir AI Copilot
short_description: Reservoir AI copilot for history-match diagnosis, visual model review and multi-agent root-cause analysis.

notes: Do not commit real API keys.
General-purpose chat requires a reachable LLM endpoint.
Reservoir demo workflows use the preloaded local demo case.
The system avoids fabricating arbitrary general knowledge if no LLM is configured.
If using an older Python version, resdata==6.2.9 may require a different compatible version.
For judging, open the ready demo dashboard directly at /demo-dashboard.
The full INTERSECT / IX import workflow is supported from the landing page but is not required for evaluating the bundled demo.
Activating a newly imported completed run triggers the deterministic diagnostics pipeline and may take a few minutes depending on model size.
The LLM final answer writer improves the readability of Ask Reservoir AI responses while keeping evidence boards, visuals and logs auditable.

agentic_architecture.summary: The system uses a semantic copilot planner that dynamically chooses whether a request should be answered directly, routed to one specialist reservoir tool, or expanded into a multi-agent diagnostic workflow. Specialist agents generate evidence, a critic ranks hypotheses, and a final synthesizer produces the user-facing answer.

routing_modes.example: Is the HW-28 water issue more likely caused by connectivity or relative permeability?

visual_evidence_policy.summary: The dashboard selects the visual evidence that best matches the user intent.

examples.question: Show TRAN multiplier corridor for HW-28
Is HW-28 water issue more likely caused by connectivity or relative permeability?
examples.visual: evidence board and hypothesis ranking table

interaction_logging.purpose: Auditability of routing, tool calls, evidence flow, critic validation and final synthesis.

recommended_questions: Give me an executive summary of the history match quality.
Why is HW-28 water mismatch happening?
Is the HW-28 water issue more likely caused by connectivity or relative permeability?
Show TRAN multiplier corridor for HW-28.
Which wells have the weakest pressure match and what do they have in common?
What model updates would you prioritize to improve the history match?
demo.judge_note: Judges are encouraged to open /demo-dashboard directly because the submitted demo case has already completed import and diagnostic post-processing.

submission_notes: The dashboard demo can be used directly after running python run.py.
Judges do not need to manually import reservoir data for the demo case.
The LLM planner enables general chat and semantic routing when Compass/OpenAI-compatible credentials are configured.
Reservoir-specific questions use specialist tools and local demo evidence.
Multi-agent diagnosis is used only when the question requires causal reasoning or hypothesis comparison.
Simple visual requests intentionally use fewer agents because over-routing would add noise rather than value.
The final official examples are diagnostic and designed to show real runtime interaction edges.
Example 4 demonstrates invalid-well guard/fallback behaviour with a required JSONL trace.
summary: Reservoir AI copilot that diagnoses history-match quality using specialist agents, visual evidence, critic-based synthesis and runtime interaction traces.

official_examples.description: Four final official examples regenerated after enabling the LLM final answer writer for cleaner user-facing synthesis.

examples.purpose: Integrated HW-28 water mismatch root-cause diagnosis
examples.answer_preview: This is a model-specific reservoir diagnosis. I compared the available profile, WCT-bias, TRAN/connectivity and property evidence before ranking the most likely causes. Main evidence: - Integrated diagnosis: For HW-28, the strongest current hypothesis is **RelPerm / endpoint saturation issue** (score 85.0). The second hypothesis is **Connectivity / TRAN issue** (score 46.7). Evidence summary: oil score 100.0, water/WCT score 20.23, gas score 98.45, BHP score 70.69. WCT pattern: Model underestimates WCT; timing/direction: breakthrough timing close / simulated too low. Profile evidence override: water-profile totals sim=6.2e+04, obs=6.96e+05. This is a... - Water-cut / spatial bias evidence: I built an interactive WCT profile for HW-28. WCT is computed as water rate divided by oil plus water rate. - Profile evidence: I loaded the relative permeability and capillary-pressure curves for HW-28 in relperm model Model_04. Select the curve group to inspect. A Krw water-mobility sensitivity is available for this well; use the Evaluate/Export button if you want to generate the modified curve candidate. Reservoir Technical review review: HW-28 also has TRAN / connectivity evidence in the smart recommendation. Do not apply a RelPerm curve change blindly if the main issue may be ... Hypothesis ranking: - Relative permeability / water mobility: support score 85.0 — oil match is good while...

llm_final_answer_writer.purpose: Polish validated specialist evidence into clear user-facing reservoir-engineering language.

examples.purpose: Ranked model-update action plan across the demo case
examples.answer_preview: Recommended model-update priorities based on the integrated evidence: 1. **Start with water/WCT behaviour**, because oil and gas matches are generally strong while water/WCT is the weakest dimension. This points to water movement, breakthrough timing, water mobility or local connectivity rather than a field-wide hydrocarbon volumetric issue. 2. **Separate connectivity/TRAN candidates from RelPerm/water-mobility candidates.** Wells where TRAN/PERM/corridor evidence is material should be tested with transmissibility or connectivity sensitivities before applying RelPerm-only tuning. 3. **Use pressure/BHP as a consistency check.** If water mismatch and BHP mismatch appear together, connectivity, pressure support or boundary/aquifer representation should be reviewed before changing only saturation functions. 4. **Prioritize local, evidence-backed edits.** Avoid broad global multipliers unless the mismatch is clearly systematic across the field. The safest workflow is to test targeted TRAN/corridor edits and RelPerm sensitivities separately, then compare impact on water, BHP, oil and gas. 5. **Preserve the good oil/gas match.** Any update that improves water but degrades oil or gas should be rejected or constrained. Supporting integrated evidence is summarized below. This is a model-specific reservoir diagnosis. I compared the available profile, WCT-bias, TRAN/connectivity and pro...

llm_final_answer_writer.purpose: Polish validated specialist evidence into clear user-facing reservoir-engineering language.

examples.answer_preview: This is a model-specific reservoir diagnosis. I compared the available profile, WCT-bias, TRAN/connectivity and property evidence before ranking the most likely causes. Main evidence: - History-match summary evidence: Created interactive distribution plot for PRESSURE from 34390 map-cell values. - Integrated diagnosis: I ranked the top 5 wells requiring model review using integrated water/WCT, BHP/pressure, gas/GOR and oil evidence. Hypothesis ranking: - profile level / timing mismatch: support score 2 — Supported by direct simulated-vs-observed water/WCT profile evidence. - pressure / BHP support or connectivity: support score 2 — Supported when BHP/pressure evidence is part of the mismatch. - local connectivity / TRAN: support score 0 — Supported when TRAN/connectivity/corridor evidence is present. - relative permeability / water mobility: support score 0 — Supported when timing/shape suggest mobility/endpoints rather than only connectivity. Most likely explanation: profile level / timing mismatch.

llm_final_answer_writer.purpose: Polish validated specialist evidence into clear user-facing reservoir-engineering language.

examples.purpose: Invalid well guard and safe fallback behaviour
examples.answer_preview: I found an explicit well name that is invalid or ambiguous for the active reservoir. I did not run the request on another well or on a default well because that would be misleading. HW-250 is not a valid well in the active dataset. Well not found in active reservoir well registry. Closest available wells: HW-25, HW-5, HW-32, HW-30, HW-29, HW-28.

final_logs.description: Clean final logs retained for submission after regenerating official examples with the LLM final answer writer enabled.

compass.note: The app reads the Compass/OpenAI-compatible endpoint from environment variables. The official checklist references https://compass.core42.ai/v1; during local testing, generation calls worked through the Compass-compatible gateway https://api.core42.ai/v1.

diagnostics_pipeline.typical_runtime: A few minutes; local tests ranged from a few minutes to approximately 5-6 minutes depending on model size.
